title: SIMBA
subtitle: Similarity search for chess decision spaces
summary: A retrieval system for finding strategically similar chess positions and supporting faster decision analysis.
longSummary: SIMBA explores how representation, indexing, and ranking quality come together in a search-heavy technical system. It is useful portfolio signal because it connects algorithms, data representation, backend thinking, evaluation, and applied AI into one explainable project.
resumeBullets: Built a large-scale similarity search system for chess positions using indexed retrieval and evaluation-aware representations.

visual.alt: Concept visual showing a chessboard connected to search and ranking signals
visual.caption: Concept visual; the live Streamlit demo is linked while a more reliable demo stack is planned.

caseStudy.problem: Chess positions create a high-dimensional comparison problem where exact matches are less useful than strategically similar examples.
approach: Represent board states with features that preserve useful decision context.
Index the search space for fast nearest-neighbor retrieval.
Evaluate retrieved positions with ranking metrics and qualitative chess relevance.
outcome: Shows algorithmic thinking across representation, retrieval, ranking, and service design.
Creates a foundation for a public demo where users can query positions and inspect similar results.
Now has an always-on portfolio landing page so the project remains accessible even when the free Streamlit demo sleeps.

title: Pharmacy Document Intelligence
subtitle: Document search and decision support
summary: A public-safe document intelligence project for searching, ranking, and explaining information across manufacturing-style documents.
longSummary: This project turns a resume experience into a public, safe, and inspectable product demo. The goal is to show how document-heavy workflows can be transformed into search, evidence review, and decision-support interfaces without exposing private or proprietary data.
resumeBullets: Built a transformer-based document intelligence system with retrieval, reranking, and deployable API workflows.

visual.alt: Concept visual showing documents, highlighted evidence, and an intelligence workflow
visual.caption: Generated concept visual for the planned public-safe rebuild.

caseStudy.problem: Document-heavy operations need fast, trustworthy ways to find evidence and make decisions without losing context.
approach: Use synthetic public-safe documents to avoid confidential data exposure.
Combine search, ranking, and evidence panels into a product-style demo.
Keep the backend deployable, testable, and easy to inspect.
outcome: Will demonstrate AI, backend, data, and UX skills in one recruiter-friendly demo.
Will produce a strong case study with architecture, metrics, tests, and screenshots.

title: Poker Behavior Modeling
subtitle: Predictive analytics for game decisions
summary: A modeling and visualization system for predicting player actions, analyzing behavior patterns, and explaining strategy signals.
longSummary: The upgraded project will frame predictive modeling as a usable analysis product: structured data pipelines, model evaluation, scenario exploration, and visual explanations for how player behavior changes across game contexts.
resumeBullets: Modeled poker action patterns with feature engineering, predictive workflows, and decision-analysis visuals.

visual.alt: Concept visual showing playing cards, prediction curves, and behavior clusters
visual.caption: Generated concept visual for the planned dashboard upgrade.

caseStudy.problem: Game logs are noisy, sequential, and difficult to interpret without feature engineering and visual feedback.
approach: Build clean features from game histories and player actions.
Compare predictive models with transparent evaluation.
Add dashboard controls that explain scenarios without requiring notebook reading.
outcome: Will broaden the portfolio with predictive analytics and interactive visualization.
Will create a clear data-science-to-product story for mainstream CS roles.

title: Augmented KGE Research
subtitle: Knowledge graph evaluation research
summary: A research pipeline for knowledge graph embeddings, link prediction, negative sampling, and retrieval-style evaluation.
longSummary: This work gives the portfolio a research signal: experimental design, model evaluation, ranking metrics, and reproducible machine-learning workflows. The future public-facing version should make the evaluation story easier to scan and understand.
resumeBullets: Built and evaluated knowledge graph embedding experiments with link-prediction metrics and reproducible PyTorch pipelines.

visual.alt: Concept visual showing a knowledge graph with ranking and evaluation signals
visual.caption: Concept visual for the research workflow.

caseStudy.problem: Knowledge graph models need careful sampling and ranking evaluation to make research results meaningful.
approach: Develop model experiments with negative sampling strategies.
Evaluate link prediction using ranking-focused metrics.
Optimize data-processing workflows for faster experimentation.
outcome: Shows research fluency alongside practical engineering implementation.
Creates a bridge between AI research, data pipelines, and measurable evaluation.
